question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower whose collateralBitmap has bit i set but collateral[i] == 0 (stale bit from a prior desync), causing the bad debt loop to call IOracle(market.collateralParams[i].oracle).price() and add 0 * price == 0 to maxDebt, under the precondition that a bitmap desync exists (bit set, collateral == 0), via the call sequence liquidate(market, j, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, ...) where j != i, violating the invariant that the liquidation loop must not include inactive collateral (collateral[i] == 0) in maxDebt, causing scoped impact: maxDebt is not inflated (0 * price == 0) but the oracle call for index i could revert, freezing liquidation of an unhealthy borrower? Proof idea: state test with stale bit (bit set, collateral == 0), assert liquidate does not revert due to oracle call for the stale bit.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator perform multiple sequential liquidations on a multi-collateral borrower, each time seizing all collateral at one index (clearing that bit), until only one collateral remains, then perform a final liquidation that seizes all remaining collateral (clearing the last bit), leaving collateralBitmap == 0 but debt > 0, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy and has multiple collaterals, via the call sequence liquidate(i, collateral[i], 0) repeated for each index, violating the invariant that after all collateral is seized the bitmap is 0 and the borrower's remaining debt is bad debt, causing scoped impact: confirm that after all bits are cleared, a subsequent liquidate(j, 0, 0) correctly realizes the remaining debt as bad debt with badDebt == debt and the bitmap loop produces maxDebt == 0? Proof idea: multi-step liquidation test, assert final state has collateralBitmap == 0 and debt == 0 after bad debt realization.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.setBit / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, setting bit i, then have an authorized operator withdraw all collateral at index i (clearing bit i), then have the borrower supply again at index i with assets > 0 (re-setting bit i), and repeat this cycle to test bitmap idempotency, under the precondition that the borrower has debt throughout, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(i, X) -> withdrawCollateral(i, X) -> supplyCollateral(i, Y) -> ..., violating the invariant that setBit and clearBit are inverses and the bitmap correctly reflects the current collateral state at each step, causing scoped impact: if any cycle leaves the bitmap in an inconsistent state, health checks could skip or double-count collateral i? Proof idea: invariant fuzz test cycling supply/withdraw at the same index, assert collateralBitmap bit i == (collateral[i] > 0) after each operation.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower create a position where the collateralBitmap has only bit 0 set (lowest index), under the precondition that the market has multiple collaterals and the borrower only supplies at index 0, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(0, assets) -> take(sell offer), violating the invariant that msb(1) == 0 (clz(1) in 256-bit context == 255, sub(255,255) == 0), causing scoped impact: if msb returns an incorrect value for bitmap == 1 (e.g., returns 255 due to clz operating on 256-bit value), the health check would access market.collateralParams[255] which is out of bounds and reverts, freezing health checks and liquidations? Proof idea: unit test msb(1) == 0, msb(2) == 1, msb(3) == 1, msb(type(uint128).max) == 127.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower call withdrawCollateral with assets == collateral[i] when debt > 0 and the health check passes (other collaterals cover the debt), clearing bit i, then immediately call withdrawCollateral at another index j with assets == collateral[j], under the precondition that after clearing bit i the remaining collateral still covers the debt, via the call sequence withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i]) -> withdrawCollateral(j, collateral[j]), violating the invariant that each withdrawal independently passes the health check with the updated bitmap, causing scoped impact: if the health check after the first withdrawal uses a stale bitmap (bit i still set), the second withdrawal might be allowed when it should be rejected, leaving the borrower undercollateralized? Proof idea: two-step withdrawal test where each step is at the health boundary, assert isHealthy is called with the correct updated bitmap after each step.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.countBits / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, where the newCollateralBitmap has exactly MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER == 16 bits set, and countBits returns 16, passing the check, but then supply at another index j with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0 in the same multicall batch, where the second call sees the bitmap already updated by the first call (since multicall uses delegatecall and shares storage), under the precondition that the borrower has 15 active collaterals and multicalls two new supplies, via the call sequence multicall([supplyCollateral(i, X), supplyCollateral(j, Y)]), violating the invariant that countBits(newCollateralBitmap) <= 16 after each supply, causing scoped impact: the second supply sees the bitmap with 16 bits set (after first supply) and newCollateralBitmap would have 17 bits, countBits returns 17 > 16, and the second supply reverts — confirm this is correctly enforced? Proof idea: multicall test with two simultaneous new-index supplies at 15-collateral capacity, assert second supply reverts with TooManyActivatedCollaterals.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower with a single active collateral at index i, where the bad debt loop computes badDebt = originalDebt - collateral[i] * price / maxLif, and then the seizedAssets/repaidUnits branch seizes all collateral[i] (clearing bit i), under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy with one active collateral, via the call sequence liquidate(market, i, collateral[i], 0, borrower, false, ...), violating the invariant that after full liquidation the bitmap is 0 and debt is reduced by repaidUnits, causing scoped impact: confirm that the bad debt loop (using local bitmap copy with bit i set) correctly computes badDebt before the storage bitmap is cleared, and the final state has collateralBitmap == 0 and debt == originalDebt - badDebt - repaidUnits? Proof idea: single-collateral full liquidation test, assert final collateralBitmap == 0 and debt == 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.setBit / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, setting bit i, but the toUint128(oldCollateral + assets) call reverts due to overflow (assets > type(uint128).max), under the precondition that assets > type(uint128).max, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(market, i, type(uint128).max + 1, borrower), violating the invariant that the bitmap bit is only set when the collateral storage is successfully updated, causing scoped impact: the revert prevents the bitmap bit from being set, so no desync occurs — confirm that the revert happens before setBit is called (since toUint128 is called first in the assignment) and the bitmap is not corrupted? Proof idea: unit test supplyCollateral with assets > type(uint128).max, assert revert and bitmap unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate with seizedAssets > collateral[collateralIndex] (attempting to seize more than available), under the precondition that collateral[collateralIndex] > 0 and seizedAssets > collateral[collateralIndex], via the call sequence liquidate(market, collateralIndex, collateral[collateralIndex] + 1, 0, borrower, false, ...), violating the invariant that collateral[collateralIndex] - seizedAssets must not underflow, causing scoped impact: Solidity 0.8+ checked arithmetic causes a revert, preventing the bitmap from being corrupted — confirm that the underflow protection prevents any bitmap desync in this path? Proof idea: unit test liquidate with seizedAssets > collateral[collateralIndex], assert revert with arithmetic underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower create a position where the collateralBitmap has bits set at indices that exceed the length of market.collateralParams (e.g., bit 5 set but market only has 5 collaterals at indices 0-4), under the precondition that a bitmap desync exists where a bit is set at an index >= market.collateralParams.length, via any path that creates such a desync, violating the invariant that all set bits in collateralBitmap must correspond to valid indices in market.collateralParams, causing scoped impact: isHealthy calls market.collateralParams[i] for bit i >= collateralParams.length, causing an array out-of-bounds revert, permanently freezing health checks, withdrawCollateral, and liquidations for that borrower? Proof idea: state test with artificially set bitmap bit >= collateralParams.length, assert isHealthy reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator perform a post-maturity liquidation (postMaturityMode == true) on a borrower with multiple active collaterals, where the LIF ramps from 1 to maxLif over TIME_TO_MAX_LIF == 15 minutes, and the bad debt loop uses the storage bitmap to compute badDebt using maxLif (not the ramped lif), under the precondition that block.timestamp is between maturity and maturity + 15 minutes, via the call sequence liquidate(market, i, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, true, ...), violating the invariant that badDebt computation must use maxLif (worst case) regardless of the current ramped lif, causing scoped impact: if the bad debt loop incorrectly uses the ramped lif instead of maxLif, badDebt is underestimated, and the loss socialization among lenders is insufficient, creating protocol insolvency? Proof idea: post-maturity liquidation test at various timestamps, assert badDebt uses maxLif not ramped lif.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.setBit / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, setting bit i, then have the market's collateral oracle for index i return a price of 0, under the precondition that the oracle is valid but returns 0, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(i, assets) -> take(sell offer to borrow), violating the invariant that isHealthy correctly computes maxDebt for all active collaterals, causing scoped impact: the health check includes bit i in the loop, calls oracle.price() == 0, and adds 0 to maxDebt — the collateral is active (bit set) but contributes 0 to health, so the borrower's health is correctly computed but the collateral is effectively worthless — confirm this does not allow the borrower to borrow against zero-price collateral? Proof idea: unit test isHealthy with one active collateral at price == 0, assert maxDebt == sum of other collaterals only.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.countBits / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, where the newCollateralBitmap is type(uint128).max (all 128 bits set), and countBits(type(uint128).max) should return 128, under the precondition that the borrower somehow has all 128 bits set (impossible via normal flow due to MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER == 16, but test the math), violating the invariant that countBits correctly returns 128 for type(uint128).max, causing scoped impact: if countBits returns a value <= 16 for type(uint128).max due to overflow, the MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER check would pass, allowing unlimited collateral activation? Proof idea: unit test countBits(type(uint128).max) == 128 and countBits(0) == 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower where the collateralBitmap has bit i set but market.collateralParams[i].oracle is address(0), causing IOracle(address(0)).price() to be called in the bad debt loop, under the precondition that the market was created with oracle == address(0) for collateral i (which should be prevented by market creation validation), via any path that creates such a state, violating the invariant that all active collateral oracles must be callable, causing scoped impact: the call to address(0).price() returns empty data and the ABI decode fails or returns 0, potentially corrupting the maxDebt computation or reverting the liquidation? Proof idea: state test with oracle == address(0) for an active collateral, assert liquidate behavior (revert or incorrect maxDebt).

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower call withdrawCollateral with assets == collateral[i] (full withdrawal) when debt > 0, clearing bit i, but the health check isHealthy is called with the updated bitmap (bit i cleared) and the updated collateral[i] == 0, under the precondition that the borrower has other collaterals that cover the debt, via the call sequence withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i]), violating the invariant that isHealthy must use the post-withdrawal state (bit i cleared, collateral[i] == 0) not the pre-withdrawal state, causing scoped impact: if isHealthy uses a stale bitmap (bit i still set), it would include collateral[i] == 0 in maxDebt (adding 0), which is harmless — but confirm the health check correctly uses the updated storage state? Proof idea: unit test withdrawCollateral full amount, assert isHealthy is called after storage update and uses updated bitmap.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower create a position where the collateralBitmap has bits set at indices i and j where i < j, and the msb-based iteration processes j first (higher index) then i, under the precondition that the borrower has two active collaterals at non-adjacent indices, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(i, X) -> supplyCollateral(j, Y) -> take(sell offer), violating the invariant that the order of iteration does not affect the sum of maxDebt (since addition is commutative), causing scoped impact: confirm that the MSB-first iteration correctly processes all set bits regardless of order, and that clearBit(bitmap, msb(bitmap)) always produces a strictly smaller bitmap? Proof idea: property test that for any nonzero uint128 bitmap, clearBit(bitmap, msb(bitmap)) < bitmap and msb(clearBit(bitmap, msb(bitmap))) < msb(bitmap) if the result is nonzero.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.setBit / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, setting bit i, then have a liquidator liquidate with seizedAssets < collateral[i] (partial seizure), leaving newCollateral > 0 and bit i still set, then have the borrower withdraw the remaining collateral[i] - seizedAssets (clearing bit i), under the precondition that the borrower is healthy after partial liquidation, via the call sequence liquidate(i, partial, 0) -> withdrawCollateral(i, remaining), violating the invariant that the bitmap correctly reflects the collateral state at each step, causing scoped impact: confirm that after partial liquidation (bit i still set, collateral[i] > 0) and subsequent full withdrawal (bit i cleared, collateral[i] == 0), the bitmap is consistent and health checks are correct? Proof idea: partial liquidation followed by full withdrawal test, assert bitmap consistency at each step.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate with seizedAssets == 0 and repaidUnits > 0 on a borrower where collateral[collateralIndex] > 0 and bit collateralIndex is set, where seizedAssets is computed as repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice) == 0 due to rounding, under the precondition that lif * repaidUnits < WAD (dust repayment), via the call sequence liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, 1, borrower, false, ...), violating the invariant that the bitmap bit for collateralIndex must be cleared when collateral[collateralIndex] reaches 0, causing scoped impact: seizedAssets rounds to 0, newCollateral == collateral[collateralIndex] > 0, condition newCollateral == 0 && seizedAssets > 0 is false, bitmap bit is not cleared — this is correct since collateral is still there — but confirm that the debt is reduced by repaidUnits and the health check is not called in liquidate (it is not), so the borrower could be left with reduced debt but same collateral? Proof idea: dust repayment liquidation test, assert debt decreases by repaidUnits and collateral and bitmap are unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower with a multi-collateral position where the bad debt loop iterates all set bits and computes badDebt, but the collateralIndex passed to liquidate is not in the bitmap (bit not set, collateral[collateralIndex] == 0), causing liquidatedCollatPrice to remain 0, and then the seizedAssets > 0 branch computes repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(0, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) == 0, under the precondition that collateral[collateralIndex] == 0 and seizedAssets > 0, via the call sequence liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 1, 0, borrower, false, ...), violating the invariant that the liquidator must repay debt proportional to seized collateral value, causing scoped impact: the liquidator seizes 1 unit of collateral for 0 debt repaid, but collateral[collateralIndex] - 1 underflows and reverts — confirm the underflow protection prevents this attack? Proof idea: unit test liquidate with collateralIndex not in bitmap and seizedAssets > 0, assert revert due to underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.countBits / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, where the newCollateralBitmap has exactly 16 bits set (passing the countBits <= 16 check), then have a liquidator liquidate and clear one bit (seizing all collateral at index j), then have the borrower supply at a new index k (setting a new bit, now 16 bits set again), under the precondition that the borrower cycles through collateral activations to maintain exactly 16 active collaterals, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(i) -> liquidate(j, all) -> supplyCollateral(k), violating the invariant that the borrower cannot exceed 16 active collaterals at any time, causing scoped impact: confirm that after liquidation clears bit j, the borrower can correctly activate a new collateral k without exceeding the limit? Proof idea: cycle test of liquidation + re-supply, assert countBits <= 16 at all times.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower create a position where the collateralBitmap has a specific pattern that causes the msb + clearBit loop in isHealthy to visit the same index twice (infinite loop or double-counting), under the precondition that clearBit(bitmap, msb(bitmap)) does not strictly reduce the bitmap for some input, via any bitmap value, violating the invariant that the while loop terminates and visits each bit exactly once, causing scoped impact: infinite loop causes out-of-gas revert on every isHealthy call, permanently freezing health checks, withdrawCollateral, and take for the borrower? Proof idea: property test that for all nonzero uint128 bitmaps, the sequence of msb + clearBit operations terminates in exactly countBits(bitmap) iterations with each index visited exactly once.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower with multiple active collaterals where the bad debt loop computes badDebt using the local _collateralBitmap snapshot, but between the snapshot and the storage bitmap update (clearBit for the liquidated collateral), a reentrant call via the liquidateCallback modifies the storage collateralBitmap (e.g., by calling supplyCollateral to set a new bit), under the precondition that the liquidator uses a malicious callback contract, via the call sequence liquidate(market, i, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, receiver, maliciousCallback, '') where maliciousCallback calls supplyCollateral(j, assets) for borrower, violating the invariant that the bitmap state used for bad debt computation must be consistent with the final storage state, causing scoped impact: the bad debt loop used the pre-callback bitmap (without bit j), but the final storage bitmap has bit j set — the bad debt was computed without j's collateral value, potentially over-realizing bad debt and over-slashing lenders? Proof idea: reentrancy test where liquidateCallback calls supplyCollateral, assert badDebt is computed correctly.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.setBit / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower supply collateral at index i with assets > 0 when oldCollateral == 0, setting bit i, then have the market's collateral oracle for index i return a price that causes collateral[i] * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD to overflow uint256 in the isHealthy loop, under the precondition that collateral[i] is near type(uint128).max and oracle price is near type(uint256).max / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, via the call sequence supplyCollateral(i, type(uint128).max) -> take(sell offer), violating the invariant that isHealthy must not revert for valid positions, causing scoped impact: the mulDivDown computation overflows and reverts, permanently freezing health checks and liquidations for that borrower? [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.clearBit / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged authorized operator call withdrawCollateral on behalf of a borrower, clearing bit i when newCollateral == 0, while the borrower simultaneously (in the same multicall) calls supplyCollateral at index i with assets > 0 (re-setting bit i), under the precondition that the operator and borrower are the same entity using multicall, via the call sequence multicall([withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i]), supplyCollateral(i, newAssets)]), violating the invariant that the bitmap correctly reflects the final collateral state after all operations, causing scoped impact: the final state has bit i set and collateral[i] == newAssets, which is consistent — but confirm the health check is called after withdrawCollateral (with bit i cleared) and passes, then supplyCollateral sets the bit again without a health check? [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: UtilsLib.msb / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator call liquidate on a borrower where the collateralBitmap has bits set at indices that are all higher than collateralIndex (the index being liquidated), causing the bad debt loop to process all bits before reaching collateralIndex (which is not in the bitmap), and liquidatedCollatPrice remains 0, under the precondition that collateralIndex is not in the bitmap but collateral[collateralIndex] > 0 (impossible in normal flow — but test the invariant), via any desync path, violating the invariant that liquidatedCollatPrice must be set to the correct oracle price for the liquidated collateral, causing scoped impact: if liquidatedCollatPrice == 0 and seizedAssets > 0, repaidUnits == 0 (free seizure) — but collateral[collateralIndex] - seizedAssets underflows and reverts if collateral[collateralIndex] == 0?
